body_txt: Overused Stock Sound Effects can screw with a TV viewer's suspension of disbelief — repeated use makes them instantly recognizable to those in the know. One of the worst offenders is the Stock Scream — one of several vocal effects used when a character has to scream and the sound the actor recorded isn't loud or piercing enough. From the popularity of some stock "scream" effects, you would think they were the only ones in existence. Currently there are about 15 commonly used stock screams, most of which have yet to be named. Looking for an Instant Wilhelm Scream? Look no further .
